Expert Interview

Dirk-Jan Wienen on Condition-Based Maintenance in Water Utilities

Home Industries Proof Resources Contact Assets

About Dirk-Jan Wienen

Dirk-Jan Wienen is an experienced maintenance engineer in the water industry with extensive knowledge of condition-based maintenance implementation in utility operations.

Interview

What are the biggest challenges in water utility maintenance?

The primary challenge is managing aging infrastructure while maintaining reliability. Many of our systems were designed decades ago and are reaching the end of their planned lifespan. Unexpected failures during peak demand periods can disrupt service to thousands of customers.

How does condition-based maintenance address these challenges?

CBM gives us visibility into actual equipment condition, allowing us to plan maintenance strategically. We can prioritize resources based on real risk rather than calendar dates. This improves reliability while reducing unnecessary maintenance costs.

What results have you seen?

We've achieved significant improvements in asset reliability and cost optimization. Equipment that was previously failing unexpectedly now operates with predictable maintenance schedules. Our teams can plan work more effectively because they know which assets need attention and when.

Advice for other utilities?

Start with your highest-value or most critical assets. Don't try to transform everything at once. Build expertise and a track record of success on pilot projects, then expand systematically. The investment in condition monitoring pays dividends through improved reliability and reduced emergency maintenance.
